{{nihongo|'''Kotoshironushi Shrine'''|事代主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

name in kana (P1814) == * ことしろぬしじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5480005003210 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '徳島県阿波市市場町伊月字宮ノ本100-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:事代主神社 (阿波市)]] {{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= 事代主神社 |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}字宮ノ本100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|事代主命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|旧郷社}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。通称は「おっべっさん」。境内は'''伊月農村公園'''として整備されている。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。一説によると{{jalink|安寧天皇|Q313345}}の伯父にあたる{{jalink|多臣}}の子孫が{{jalink|出雲族|Q22123990}}を率いて当地の{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}伊月にたどり着き、祖神である{{jalink|事代主命}}と祖母の{{jalink|五十鈴依媛命|Q10881557}}を奉斎したと伝わる。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は事代明神、事代大明神と称していた。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|事代主命}} === 境内社 === * 鎮守神社 * 森壱岐神 * 大住大明神 === 交通 === * {{jalink|徳島自動車道|Q11490216}}「{{jalink|土成インターチェンジ|Q10927770}}」より車で約15分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= 事代主神社 |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}字宮ノ本100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|事代主命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|旧郷社}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。通称は「おっべっさん」。境内は'''伊月農村公園'''として整備されている。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。一説によると{{jalink|安寧天皇|Q313345}}の伯父にあたる{{jalink|多臣}}の子孫が{{jalink|出雲族|Q22123990}}を率いて当地の{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}伊月にたどり着き、祖神である{{jalink|事代主命}}と祖母の{{jalink|五十鈴依媛命|Q10881557}}を奉斎したと伝わる。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は事代明神、事代大明神と称していた。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|事代主命}} === 境内社 === * 鎮守神社 * 森壱岐神 * 大住大明神 === 交通 === * {{jalink|徳島自動車道|Q11490216}}「{{jalink|土成インターチェンジ|Q10927770}}」より車で約15分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

name in kana (P1814) == * ことしろぬしじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5480005003210 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '徳島県阿波市市場町伊月字宮ノ本100-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:事代主神社 (阿波市)]] {{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= 事代主神社 |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}字宮ノ本100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|事代主命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|旧郷社}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。通称は「おっべっさん」。境内は'''伊月農村公園'''として整備されている。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。一説によると{{jalink|安寧天皇|Q313345}}の伯父にあたる{{jalink|多臣}}の子孫が{{jalink|出雲族|Q22123990}}を率いて当地の{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}伊月にたどり着き、祖神である{{jalink|事代主命}}と祖母の{{jalink|五十鈴依媛命|Q10881557}}を奉斎したと伝わる。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は事代明神、事代大明神と称していた。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|事代主命}} === 境内社 === * 鎮守神社 * 森壱岐神 * 大住大明神 === 交通 === * {{jalink|徳島自動車道|Q11490216}}「{{jalink|土成インターチェンジ|Q10927770}}」より車で約15分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= 事代主神社 |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}字宮ノ本100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|事代主命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|旧郷社}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。通称は「おっべっさん」。境内は'''伊月農村公園'''として整備されている。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。一説によると{{jalink|安寧天皇|Q313345}}の伯父にあたる{{jalink|多臣}}の子孫が{{jalink|出雲族|Q22123990}}を率いて当地の{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}伊月にたどり着き、祖神である{{jalink|事代主命}}と祖母の{{jalink|五十鈴依媛命|Q10881557}}を奉斎したと伝わる。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は事代明神、事代大明神と称していた。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|事代主命}} === 境内社 === * 鎮守神社 * 森壱岐神 * 大住大明神 === 交通 === * {{jalink|徳島自動車道|Q11490216}}「{{jalink|土成インターチェンジ|Q10927770}}」より車で約15分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= 事代主神社 |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}字宮ノ本100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|事代主命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|旧郷社}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。通称は「おっべっさん」。境内は'''伊月農村公園'''として整備されている。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。一説によると{{jalink|安寧天皇|Q313345}}の伯父にあたる{{jalink|多臣}}の子孫が{{jalink|出雲族|Q22123990}}を率いて当地の{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}伊月にたどり着き、祖神である{{jalink|事代主命}}と祖母の{{jalink|五十鈴依媛命|Q10881557}}を奉斎したと伝わる。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は事代明神、事代大明神と称していた。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|事代主命}} === 境内社 === * 鎮守神社 * 森壱岐神 * 大住大明神 === 交通 === * {{jalink|徳島自動車道|Q11490216}}「{{jalink|土成インターチェンジ|Q10927770}}」より車で約15分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}

拝殿 |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}字宮ノ本100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|事代主命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|旧郷社}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。通称は「おっべっさん」。境内は'''伊月農村公園'''として整備されている。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。一説によると{{jalink|安寧天皇|Q313345}}の伯父にあたる{{jalink|多臣}}の子孫が{{jalink|出雲族|Q22123990}}を率いて当地の{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}伊月にたどり着き、祖神である{{jalink|事代主命}}と祖母の{{jalink|五十鈴依媛命|Q10881557}}を奉斎したと伝わる。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は事代明神、事代大明神と称していた。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|事代主命}} === 境内社 === * 鎮守神社 * 森壱岐神 * 大住大明神 === 交通 === * {{jalink|徳島自動車道|Q11490216}}「{{jalink|土成インターチェンジ|Q10927770}}」より車で約15分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}

name in kana (P1814) == * ことしろぬしじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5480005003210 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '徳島県阿波市市場町伊月字宮ノ本100-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:事代主神社 (阿波市)]] {{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= 事代主神社 |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}字宮ノ本100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|事代主命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|旧郷社}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。通称は「おっべっさん」。境内は'''伊月農村公園'''として整備されている。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。一説によると{{jalink|安寧天皇|Q313345}}の伯父にあたる{{jalink|多臣}}の子孫が{{jalink|出雲族|Q22123990}}を率いて当地の{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}伊月にたどり着き、祖神である{{jalink|事代主命}}と祖母の{{jalink|五十鈴依媛命|Q10881557}}を奉斎したと伝わる。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は事代明神、事代大明神と称していた。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|事代主命}} === 境内社 === * 鎮守神社 * 森壱岐神 * 大住大明神 === 交通 === * {{jalink|徳島自動車道|Q11490216}}「{{jalink|土成インターチェンジ|Q10927770}}」より車で約15分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}

name in kana (P1814) == * ことしろぬしじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5480005003210 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '徳島県阿波市市場町伊月字宮ノ本100-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:事代主神社 (阿波市)]] {{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= 事代主神社 |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}字宮ノ本100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|事代主命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|旧郷社}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''事代主神社'''（ことしろぬしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町伊月|Q55523753}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。通称は「おっべっさん」。境内は'''伊月農村公園'''として整備されている。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。一説によると{{jalink|安寧天皇|Q313345}}の伯父にあたる{{jalink|多臣}}の子孫が{{jalink|出雲族|Q22123990}}を率いて当地の{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}伊月にたどり着き、祖神である{{jalink|事代主命}}と祖母の{{jalink|五十鈴依媛命|Q10881557}}を奉斎したと伝わる。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は事代明神、事代大明神と称していた。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|事代主命}} === 境内社 === * 鎮守神社 * 森壱岐神 * 大住大明神 === 交通 === * {{jalink|徳島自動車道|Q11490216}}「{{jalink|土成インターチェンジ|Q10927770}}」より車で約15分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Awa|Q791587}}{{jalink|Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・{{jalink|former district shrine}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} enshrined in {{jalink|Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753}}, {{jalink|Awa|Q791587}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{jalink|Takeomi}}, maternal grandfather of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}}, led the {{jalink|Izumo clan|Q22123990}} to Izuki in {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{jalink|Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{jalink|Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}

In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{jalink|Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Awa|Q791587}}{{jalink|Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・{{jalink|former district shrine}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} enshrined in {{jalink|Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753}}, {{jalink|Awa|Q791587}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{jalink|Takeomi}}, maternal grandfather of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}}, led the {{jalink|Izumo clan|Q22123990}} to Izuki in {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{jalink|Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{jalink|Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Awa|Q791587}}{{jalink|Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・{{jalink|former district shrine}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} enshrined in {{jalink|Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753}}, {{jalink|Awa|Q791587}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{jalink|Takeomi}}, maternal grandfather of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}}, led the {{jalink|Izumo clan|Q22123990}} to Izuki in {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{jalink|Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{jalink|Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Awa|Q791587}}{{jalink|Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・{{jalink|former district shrine}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} enshrined in {{jalink|Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753}}, {{jalink|Awa|Q791587}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{jalink|Takeomi}}, maternal grandfather of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}}, led the {{jalink|Izumo clan|Q22123990}} to Izuki in {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{jalink|Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{jalink|Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Awa|Q791587}}{{jalink|Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・{{jalink|former district shrine}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} enshrined in {{jalink|Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753}}, {{jalink|Awa|Q791587}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{jalink|Takeomi}}, maternal grandfather of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}}, led the {{jalink|Izumo clan|Q22123990}} to Izuki in {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{jalink|Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{jalink|Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}[[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Awa|Q791587}}{{jalink|Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・{{jalink|former district shrine}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} enshrined in {{jalink|Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753}}, {{jalink|Awa|Q791587}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{jalink|Takeomi}}, maternal grandfather of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}}, led the {{jalink|Izumo clan|Q22123990}} to Izuki in {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{jalink|Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557}}. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{jalink|Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill||lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}・{{ill||lt=former district shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill||lt=Takeomi|UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill||lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill||lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|1=UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|1=UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|1=UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|1=UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|1=UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990|1=Izumo zoku}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557|1=Isuzuyori-hime}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770|1=Donari interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * ことしろぬしじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5480005003210 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '徳島県阿波市市場町伊月字宮ノ本100-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:事代主神社 (阿波市)]] {{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|1=UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990|1=Izumo zoku}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557|1=Isuzuyori-hime}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770|1=Donari interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}[[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|1=UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990|1=Izumo zoku}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557|1=Isuzuyori-hime}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770|1=Donari interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことしろぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)}}[[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * ことしろぬしじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5480005003210 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '徳島県阿波市市場町伊月字宮ノ本100-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:事代主神社 (阿波市)]] {{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|1=UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990|1=Izumo zoku}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557|1=Isuzuyori-hime}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770|1=Donari interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|1=UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990|1=Izumo zoku}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557|1=Isuzuyori-hime}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770|1=Donari interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]]

Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|1=UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990|1=Izumo zoku}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557|1=Isuzuyori-hime}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770|1=Donari interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|1=UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990|1=Izumo zoku}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557|1=Isuzuyori-hime}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770|1=Donari interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758503}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称= Kotoshironushi Shrine |画像= [[File:Kotoshironushi Shrine, Awa City.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}} Aza Miyanomoto 100-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=事代主神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' (ことしろぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Ichiba-chō Izuki|Q55523753|WD=Q55523753|1=Ichiba-chō Izuki}}, {{ill|lt=Awa|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is popularly called "Obbe-san." The precinct is maintained as '''Izuki Rural Park'''. === History === The founding year is unknown. One tradition says that descendants of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeomi|1=UNKNOWN}}, maternal grandfather of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}}, led the {{ill|lt=Izumo clan|Q22123990|WD=Q22123990|1=Izumo zoku}} to Izuki in {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} and enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} together with ancestress {{ill|lt=Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto|Q10881557|WD=Q10881557|1=Isuzuyori-hime}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Kotoji Myōjin or Kotoji Daimyōjin. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Chinju Shrine * Moroshiki no Kami * Ōzumi Daimyōjin === Access === * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsuchinari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770|1=Donari interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kotoshironushi Shrine (Q48758503)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]]
